page: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb is a memory-optimized server instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage, alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% with a score of 3621.37. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier levels. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is more moderate. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score fall into the average category, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, landing in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform within average parameters.

This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient solution for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. The combination of a high memory capacity with shared vCPUs makes it suitable for caching layers, in-memory databases like Redis, and moderate web serving. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded parallel computing or large-scale LLM inference due to its shared CPU allocation and average multi-core benchmark results.
description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, it offers a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, paired with 1 GB of network-attached storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance, though multi-core and multi-threaded operations remain average to weak. This resource configuration provides a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand dedicated CPU cores. It is highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and general web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
